speaker: Narrator
text: Everything’s been packed into the car. Blanche takes her seat on the right. In a vain attempt to hide from Blanche's eyeline, Christina slumps over the fish cooler in the back seat.

speaker: Narrator
text: Blanche furrows her eyebrows while looking at Christina through the rear view mirror. She'd probably have to drive her through the city back to her place instead of the university.

speaker: Blanche
text: Christina, do you really think you can take the metro home?

speaker: Narrator
text: Despite her condition, she can't help but feel patronized. In her mind she still feels she is capable of walking.

speaker: Christina
text: I'm fine... really.

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina feels something coming up her throat and she forces it down, creating a loud, audible gulp. Blanche sighs.

speaker: Blanche
text: Listen, I'm no fool, I know you're sick back there. You're in no condition to go back to the university. I'll drop you off at your place and bring the fish back for study.

speaker: Christina
text: Thanks, it must be a big burden on you to drive me home... Blanche...

speaker: Christina
text: Especially... with the cost of p-petrol these days... and the... traffic...

speaker: Blanche
text: It's times like these that I'm happy that my parents raised me to be so kind to the unfortunate.

speaker: Christina
text: Mm hmm.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Okay, ahem. [wave]That'll be all Chatterpillars![/wave]

speaker: Narrator
text: A long groan is forced out from Christina’s throat. From the corner of her eye, Blanche can see the distress in Christina’s body as she languidly reaches into her pocket and pulls out her phone.

speaker: Narrator
text:
She weakly handles her phone while her head slumps over onto the screen. Whether it’s the residual water from the river or her sweat, drops of something are falling on the image of Pasaya. 
	
	Christina’s breathing intensifies whenever she sees her avatar's ample breasts shake.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I always knew the hive loved karaoke night!

speaker: Pasaya
text: I love all of you so, so, so, soooooo much for coming here today. Especially all supporters who’ve floored me with requests!

speaker: Pasaya
text: Oh Gystf320, thank you for the dono!

speaker: Pasaya
text: Chatterpillars, before I go, I will leave you guys with a question. Would you guys still love me if I was a worm?

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina leaned closer to her phone. From the driver’s seat, all Blanche could see was Christina dangling over her seatbelt, breathing as if she had run a Marathon. 
	
	The visage periodically gets broken up by intense fits of coughing.

speaker: Blanche
text: You better not be dying in my car back there!

speaker: Blanche
text: Christina?

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina’s limps up like a zombie rising from its grave. Her face is covered in liquid, drool from her mouth, snot from her nose, and sweat on her forehead.
	
	Her eyes are are puffy and red from irritation.

speaker: Blanche
text: Do you think there must be a better way to get back to your building?

speaker: Christina
text: Try-

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina's words are interrupted with a flurry of hacks, gasps, and wheezes. She tries to speak again, but only a strained breathless voice exits her body.

speaker: Christina
text: [shake]Oh god, pull over, pull over.[/shake]

speaker: Blanche
text: Where?!

speaker: Narrator
text: Blanche starts to floor it, weaving in and out of traffic, trying to get off the highway as fast as possible. Maybe there would a place to pull over around here?

speaker: Narrator
text:
She finds one. There was a patch of greenery, a park, with a trash can. A few kids are kicking a ball around with a little dog joining in the fun. She stops in the bike lane. 
	
	Christina is just barely holding back the urge to puke with her palm against her mouth. She frantically pulls open the door of her sedan, finds a nearby bush, and releases with a hearty "Blaaargh!".

speaker: Narrator
text: She turns around to look at Blanche with a drop or two still on her lip. She notices from the reflection on Blanche's car

speaker: Christina
text: Oh. My bad.

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina brushes it off her face and wipes it on the bush. Slowly, she hobbles back to the car and sits down again, resting her head against the cooler filled with fish.

speaker: Blanche
text: So do you feel better now?

speaker: Christina
text: Yeah, I think so.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Aside from the post-nausua drowsiness, Christina seemed to have made a miraculous recovery. Was it just a fluke?
	
	Blanche exhales in relief after the stress of the situation died down. She didn't want an emergency room visit when they were trying to pass a final assignment in a class, or worse, a dead classmate.
	
	Just to be safe, they decide that Blanche should still drive Christina back to her apartment. It’s not a long drive from the park to Christina's place from where they are now.
	
	To lighten the mood, Blanche asks a question...

speaker: Blanche
text: Christina, what kind of music do you listen to?

speaker: Christina
text: Dunno, anything really.

speaker: Narrator
text: Blanche turns the radio dial with the rim of her hand swiftly. It’s already dialed into the good metal station where a song about the Battle of Ain Jalut just at the point where the Egyptian Mamluks start beating the Mongols ass.

speaker: Blanche
text: My fucking shit!

speaker: Narrator
text:
The music rocks on for an hour or so as the car continues its journey.
	
	Blanche hates driving to her place. It makes her feel like she's driving to Helsinki with how far it is, among other reasons.
